title: MAPseq (Multiplexed Analysis of Projections by Sequencing) sample processing protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"dqeaf","text":"This protocol describes sample processing steps of MAPseq, a high-throughput mapping of single-neuron projections by sequencing of barcoded RNA, as described in details by Kebschull et al.., 2016 . In MAPseq, a brain area of interest is infected with a Sindbis barcoded library. After 40-44 hrs, the injection and the projection sites of interest are dissected, processed and sequenced using this protocol.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"fko0o","text":"Extra caution should be taken to avoid cross sample contamination during RNA extraction and reverse transcription.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7hqio","text":"Use PCR II test to decide the optimal PCR II cycles. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2opu0","text":"Try to use the least PCR II cycle to minimize the template switching rate.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fuq3b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

67910080F011EBB1E93D6B3469564F","url":"https://www.thermofisher.com/order/catalog/product/12346086?SID=srch-hj-12346086#/12346086?SID=srch-hj-12346086"}},"7":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"47F46F0080F011EBB1E93D6B3469564F","url":"https://www.promega.com/products/nucleic-acid-extraction/clean-up-and-concentration/wizard-sv-gel-and-pcr-clean-up-system/?catNum=A9282"}},"8":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"41A57C1080F111EBB1E93D6B3469564F","url":"https://www.agilent.com/en/product/automated-electrophoresis/bioanalyzer-systems/bioanalyzer-dna-kits-reagents/bioanalyzer-high-sensitivity-dna-analysis-228249"}},"9":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"EBEAFDB085D011EB9D2CEB9EBAA78F9C","url":"https://www.qiagen.com/us/products/discovery-and-translational-research/dna-rna-purification/dna-purification/dna-clean-up/minelute-gel-extraction-kit/#orderinginformation"}}}}
steps: Tissues are homogenized in 400ul of Trizol either with a Pellet Pestle Motor or with a tissue homogenizer, and then add 600ul of Trizol to make 1 ml of Trizol/sample Extract RNA according to manufacturer’s protocol of Trizol Reagent Dissolve RNA of each tissue sample into 13 ul of H2O

One reaction per sample. For each reaction, add the following reagents(total of 13ul) into each well: 4 ul of RNA from each sample 6 ul of H2O 1 ul of spike-in RNA (Spike-in RNA has its unique barcode and is used to normalize for varying reverse transcription, PCR or library making efficiencies) 1ul of 10uM RT primer (each RT primer contains a random 12-nt unique molecular identifier (UMI) to tag each individual barcode mRNA molecule and a 8-nt slice specific identifier (SSI) to tag each sample) 1 ul of 10mM dNTP Mix well.
Each fresh frozen brain area of interest is dissected and kept in RNAse-free tubes at -80°C before RNA extraction.
Incubate at 70 °C for 10 minutes, then immediately transfer samples to ice. Incubate on ice for 5 minutes.
Add per well (total of 7ul) 4ul SSIV buffer 1ul 0.1M DTT 1ul RNasin 1ul SSIV Mix well.
Incubate the mixture in a thermocycler 10min at 55 °C 10min at 80 °C Store samples at 4 °C or -20ºC till next step.
Pool all of the RT product from targets or injection sites. Do not mix target site with injection site. Mix well.

For pooled samples from target sites Add 1.8X AMPure XP beads Mix by pipetting 10 times Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature Aliquot into several tubes for easier elution Put on magnetic rack, wait for 2-3 minutes till solution is clear On magnetic rack, discard supernatant Wash twice with fresh 80% EtOH to cover the pellet for 30 seconds Air dry pellet till Resuspend beads in (17 * (total number of target site/10) ul) of H20 Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes On magnetic rack, collect all of supernatant in an Eppendorf tube.

For pooled samples from injection sites Add 1.8X AMPure XP beads Mix by pipetting 10 times Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature Aliquot into several tubes for easier elution Put on magnetic rack, wait for 2-3 minutes till solution is clear On magnetic rack, discard supernatant Wash twice with fresh 80% EtOH to cover the pellet for 30 seconds Air dry pellet Resuspend beads in (17 * (total number of target site/5) ul) of H20 Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes On magnetic rack, collect all of supernatant in an Eppendorf tube.
Per 17ul of bead purified product, add 2.4ul SSIV buffer 0.6ul 0.1M DTT 5.6ul Second Strand buffer 0.75ul 10mM dNTPs 0.25ul E coli DNA ligase 1ul DNA polymerase I 0.25ul RNaseH Mix well.
Incubate the mixture in a thermocycler 2 hours at 16ºC Add 1ul of T4 DNA polymerase Incubate for 10 minutes at 16ºC. Store sample at 4ºC or -20ºC till next step.

Pool all 2 nd stranding reactions, measure the total volume Add 1.8x AMPure XP beads Mix by pipetting 10 times Incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature Put on magnetic rack, wait for 2-3 minutes till solution is clear On magnetic rack, discard supernatant Wash twice with fresh 80% EtOH to cover the pellet for 30 seconds Air dry pellet Resuspend beads in (16 * (total number of 2nd strand reaction) ul) of H20 Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes On magnetic rack, collect all of supernatant in a PCR tube.
Per 16ul of beads purified product, add 2ul Exonuclease buffer 2ul Exo I Mix well.
Incubate the mixture in a thermocycler 1 hr at 37 ºC Heat inactivate at 80 ºC for 20 minutes Store sample at 4ºC or -20ºC till next step.

Per 20ul Exonuclease reaction, add 25ul Accuprime buffer 25ul 10uM nested1st gfpF primer 25ul 10uM nested2nd primer 2.5 ul Accuprime Pfx HF enzyme 152.5ul H20 Mix well and aliquot the mixture into PCR tubes
Perform thermal cycling as follows: Initial denaturation at 95ºC 2 minutes Run 15 cycles of 95ºC 15 seconds 68ºC 2.5 minutes Final extension at 68ºC 5 minutes
Add 5 ul of Exo I to each 50 ul PCR reaction Incubation at 37ºC for 30 minutes Heat inactivation at 80ºC for 20 minutes

Test optimal PCR II cycle number in a 25ul reaction/test by adding 2.5ul of 1/10 diluted PCR I product 2.5ul Accuprime buffer 2.5ul 10uM Sol I primer 2.5ul 10uM Sol II primer 0.25ul Accuprime Pfx HF enzyme 14.75ul H20

For pooled target sites, run 14, 17, 20, 23, 26 and 29 cycles For pooled injection sites, run 11, 14, 17, 20, and 23 cycles Perform thermal cycling as follows: Initial denaturation at 95ºC 2 minutes Run different cycles of 95ºC 15 seconds 68ºC 1 minutes Final extension at 68ºC 5 minutes
Load test PCR II products on 2% agarose gel to find out the lowest cycle number which could generate a single and clean 230bp band.
For pooled injection sites, use 12 ml of PCR II reaction For pooled target sites, use 3 ml of PCR II reaction for <12 samples, 6 ml for <40 samples, and 12 ml for >40 samples.
Perform thermal cycling as follows: Initial denaturation at 95ºC 2 minutes Run optimal cycles of 95ºC 15 seconds 68ºC 2.5 minutes Final extension at 68ºC 5 minutes

In each PCR II reaction, Add the same concentration of PCR I product as what is used in the test PCR II 1x Accuprime buffer 1uM Sol I primer 1uM Sol II primer 1U of Accuprime Pfx HF enzyme/50ul of reaction
Purify PCR product using Wizard® SV Gel and PCR Clean-Up System from Promega and elute the product into 40ul of H2O per 1ml of PCR product
Load purified PCR product into 2% agarose gel
Cut ~230bp band from the gel and purify the product with MinElute Gel Extraction Kit from Qiagen
Load purified 230bp PCR product on a DNA bioanalyzer chip using Agilent High Sensitivity DNA Kit to confirm its size and quantity before submitting for sequencing

Submit purified 230bp product for an Illumina NextSeq500 high output run at paired end 36 using the SBS3T sequencing primer for paired end 1 and the Illumina small RNA sequencing primer 2 for paired end 2.
creator: Huiqing Zhan
